common.name: Name
common.maintenanceServerIssues: Server is experiencing issues. Some features may be unavailable.
common.title: Title
common.addFirstProviderDesc: Connect an AI provider to start routing requests through OmniRoute. You can use free providers, API keys, or OAuth accounts.
common.freeTierProvidersDesc: Providers with free tiers — some require an API key signup, others need no credentials at all.

sidebar.serverDisconnectedMsg: The proxy server has been stopped or is restarting.

header.endpointDescription: Minimal API endpoints, provider management, and routing combos
header.apiManagerDescription: Manage API keys and access control for your OmniRoute instance
header.analyticsDescription: Usage charts, token trends, and request analytics
header.costsDescription: Track spending, analyze trends, and manage your AI budget across all providers
header.logsDescription: Request logs, errors, and completed call details
header.healthDescription: System health overview: providers, circuit breakers, rate limits, and database
header.settingsGeneralDescription: General runtime settings for the local router
header.changelogDescription: Release notes for retained router functionality

home.quickStartDesc: Get up and running in 4 steps. Connect providers, route models, monitor everything.
home.step1Desc: Go to <endpoint>Endpoint</endpoint> -> Registered Keys. Generate one key per environment.
home.step2Desc: Add accounts in <providers>Providers</providers>. Supports OAuth, API Key, and free tiers.
home.step3Desc: Set base URL to {url} in your IDE or API client.
home.step4Desc: Track tokens, cost and errors in <logs>Request Logs</logs> and <analytics>Analytics</analytics>.
home.noModelsAvailable: No models available for this provider.
home.updateAvailableDesc: A new version is available. Click to update.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.diversityScoreDesc: Provider concentration snapshot for the recent traffic window.
analytics.diversityHigherExplanation: Higher values mean traffic is spread across multiple providers.
analytics.overviewDescription: Monitor your API usage patterns, token consumption, costs, and activity trends across all providers and models.
analytics.evalsDescription: Run evaluation suites to test and validate your LLM endpoints. Compare model quality, detect regressions, and benchmark latency.
analytics.utilizationDescription: Provider quota usage trends and rate limit tracking
analytics.comboHealthDescription: Combo-level quota, usage distribution, and performance metrics

apiManager.title: API Keys
apiManager.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this API key?
apiManager.noKeysDesc: Create your first API key to authenticate requests to your endpoint
apiManager.keyManagementDesc: Create and manage API keys for authenticating requests to your endpoint
apiManager.keysSecurityNote: Each key isolates usage tracking and can be revoked independently. Keys are masked after creation for security.
apiManager.name: Name
apiManager.tipAuth: Use API keys in the Authorization header as Bearer YOUR_KEY
apiManager.tipSecure: Keys are only shown once during creation — store them securely
apiManager.tipSeparate: Create separate keys for different clients or environments
apiManager.tipRestrict: Restrict keys to specific models for better security and cost control
apiManager.keyNameDesc: Choose a descriptive name to identify this key's purpose
apiManager.managementAccessDesc: Allow this API key to manage OmniRoute configuration.
apiManager.selfServiceVisibilityDesc: Control what this key can see about its own usage and shared upstream quota.
apiManager.ownUsageVisibilityDesc: Allow this key to call its status endpoint for its own USD usage, budget percent, and token totals.
apiManager.sharedAccountQuotaVisibilityDesc: Allow this key to see shared upstream account quota when one explicit connection is configured.
apiManager.localUsageCommandDesc: Allows this API key to use @@om-usage to retrieve cached usage and quota information without calling an upstream provider.
apiManager.keyCreatedNote: Copy and store this key now — it won't be shown again.
apiManager.allEndpointsAllowed: This key can access all API endpoints.
apiManager.endpointsRestricted: Restricted to {count} endpoint{count, plural, one {} other {s}}.
apiManager.autoResolveDesc: Auto-resolve ambiguous model names to native provider for this API key.
apiManager.streamDefaultModeDesc: Controls omitted `stream` flags for this key. JSON mode returns non-streaming responses unless the client explicitly requests SSE.
apiManager.keyActiveDesc: Enable or disable this API key. Disabled keys are immediately rejected with 403.
apiManager.accessScheduleDesc: Restrict access to specific hours and days of the week.
apiManager.scheduleTimezoneHint: Use IANA timezone names, e.g. America/New_York, Europe/Berlin
apiManager.allowAllInfo: This key can access all available models.
apiManager.restrictInfo: This key can access {selected} of {total} models.
apiManager.searchModels: Search models by name or provider...
apiManager.keyNameTooLong: Key name must be {max} characters or less
apiManager.keyNameInvalid: Key name can only contain letters, numbers, spaces, hyphens, and underscores
apiManager.failedCreateKeyRetry: Failed to create key. Please try again.
apiManager.failedDeleteKeyRetry: Failed to delete key. Please try again.
apiManager.failedUpdatePermissionsRetry: Failed to update permissions. Please try again.
apiManager.keyOnlyAvailableAtCreation: Full key available only at creation time — copy it when you first create the key
apiManager.regenerateConfirm: Are you sure you want to regenerate this API key? The old key will be immediately invalidated.
apiManager.failedRegenerateKeyRetry: Failed to regenerate API key. Please try again.
apiManager.allowAllDesc: This key can access all available models.
apiManager.restrictDesc: This key can access {selectedCount} of {totalModels} models.
apiManager.apiManagerCustomRateLimitsDesc: Override global default limits. Leave empty to use defaults.
apiManager.disableNonPublicModelsDesc: Reject requests for models that are not discovered or not marked as public in the provider catalog

combos.title: Combos
combos.description: Create model combos with weighted routing and fallback support
combos.autoCatalogDescription: Built-in auto/* combos resolved dynamically from connected providers. Use any of these IDs as the model field — no setup needed.
combos.priorityDesc: Sequential fallback: tries model 1 first, then 2, etc.
combos.weightedDesc: Distributes traffic by weight percentage with fallback
combos.roundRobinDesc: Circular distribution: each request goes to the next model in rotation
combos.contextRelayDesc: Preserves session continuity with handoff summaries when accounts rotate
combos.randomDesc: Uniform random selection, then fallback to remaining models
combos.leastUsedDesc: Picks the model with fewest requests, balancing load over time
combos.costOptimizedDesc: Routes to the cheapest model first based on pricing
combos.resetAwareDesc: Balances remaining quota against 5h and weekly resets, then round-robins similar scores
combos.strictRandomDesc: Shuffle deck — uses each model once before reshuffling
combos.contextRelayHandoffThresholdHelp: When quota usage reaches this threshold, OmniRoute generates a structured handoff summary before the active account is exhausted.
combos.contextRelayMaxMessagesHelp: Limits how much recent history is condensed into the relay summary.
combos.contextRelaySummaryModelHelp: Optional override model used only for generating the handoff summary. Leave empty to reuse the active combo model.
combos.contextRelayProviderNote: Context Relay currently generates handoffs for Codex account rotation. Pair it with multiple accounts of the same provider for the best continuity.
combos.advancedHint: Leave empty to use global defaults. These override per-provider settings.

priority.when: You have one preferred model and only want fallback on failure.
priority.avoid: You need request distribution across models.
priority.example: Primary coding model with cheaper backup for outages.

weighted.when: You need controlled traffic split across models.
weighted.avoid: You cannot maintain accurate weights over time.

round-robin.when: You want predictable and even distribution.
round-robin.avoid: Models differ too much in latency or cost.
round-robin.example: Same model on multiple accounts to spread throughput.

random.when: You want simple distribution with minimal setup.

least-used.when: You want adaptive balancing based on live demand.
least-used.avoid: Traffic is too low to benefit from usage balancing.
least-used.example: Mixed workloads where one model often gets overloaded.

cost-optimized.when: Cost reduction is your top priority.
cost-optimized.avoid: Pricing data is missing or outdated.
cost-optimized.example: Background or batch jobs where lower cost is preferred.

reset-aware.when: You route across multiple accounts with quota telemetry and different reset windows.
reset-aware.avoid: Quota telemetry is unavailable for most accounts.
reset-aware.example: Prefer a 60% weekly account resetting tomorrow over an 80% account resetting later.

strict-random.when: Use when you want perfectly even spread — each model used once before repeating.
strict-random.avoid: Avoid when models have different quality or latency and order matters.
strict-random.example: Example: Multiple accounts of the same model to distribute usage evenly.

p2c.when: Use when you want low-latency selection using Power-of-Two-Choices algorithm.
p2c.avoid: Avoid for small combos with 2 or fewer models — no benefit over round-robin.
p2c.example: Example: High-throughput inference across 4+ equivalent model endpoints.

context-relay.when: Use when long sessions must survive account rotation without losing the working context.
context-relay.avoid: Avoid when account switching is rare or when you do not want extra summarization requests.
context-relay.example: Example: Codex sessions that rotate across multiple accounts near quota exhaustion.

fill-first.when: Use when you want to fully exhaust one provider's quota before moving to the next.
fill-first.avoid: Avoid when you need request-level load balancing across providers.
fill-first.example: Example: Use all $200 Deepgram credits before falling back to Groq.

auto.when: Use when you need multi-factor scoring routing based on cost, latency, and quality.
auto.avoid: Avoid when you need strict priority ordering or historical persistence.
auto.example: Example: Balance requests across models with different strengths.

lkgp.when: Use when you want to route based on historical success rates and performance.
lkgp.avoid: Avoid when historical data is limited or unreliable.
lkgp.example: Example: Route to models with a good track record on specific tasks.

context-optimized.when: Use when you need to optimize context window usage across models.
context-optimized.avoid: Avoid when models have similar context lengths or tasks are simple.
context-optimized.example: Example: Distribute long conversations across models with larger context windows.

advancedHelp.maxRetries: How many retries are attempted before failing a request.
advancedHelp.retryDelay: Initial wait between retries. Higher values reduce burst pressure.
advancedHelp.healthcheck: Skips unhealthy models/providers from routing decisions.
advancedHelp.concurrencyPerModel: Max simultaneous requests allowed per model in round-robin.
advancedHelp.queueTimeout: How long a request can wait in queue before timing out.
advancedHelp.failoverBeforeRetry: When enabled, any upstream error triggers immediate failover to the next combo target, skipping all retries and fallback URLs.
advancedHelp.maxSetRetries: Number of times to retry the full target set when every target fails. 0 = no set-level retry.
advancedHelp.setRetryDelayMs: Delay between set-level retry attempts, giving transient issues time to resolve.

combos.templatesDescription: Apply a starting profile, then adjust models and config.
combos.templateHighAvailabilityDesc: Priority routing with health checks and safe retries.
combos.templateBalancedDesc: Least-used routing to spread demand over time.
combos.quickTestDescription: Run a test now to confirm fallback and latency behavior.
combos.pricingCoverageHint: Cost-optimized works best when all combo models have pricing.
combos.warningRoundRobinSingleModel: Round-robin is most useful with at least 2 models.
combos.warningCostOptimizedPartialPricing: Only {priced} of {total} models have pricing. Routing may be partially cost-aware.
combos.warningCostOptimizedNoPricing: No pricing data found for this combo. Cost-optimized may route unexpectedly.
combos.filterEmptyTitle: No combos match this strategy filter.
combos.filterEmptyIntelligentDescription: Create an auto or LKGP combo to populate the intelligent routing dashboard.
combos.filterEmptyDeterministicDescription: Only auto and LKGP combos exist right now. Switch back to All or create a deterministic combo.
combos.readinessDescription: Review the checklist before creating or updating this combo.
combos.readinessCheckModels: At least one model is selected
combos.saveBlockedTitle: Save is blocked until the following items are fixed:
combos.saveBlockPricing: Add pricing for at least one model or choose a different strategy.
combos.intelligentPanelDesc: Real-time scoring and health status for this auto-routing combo.
combos.configOnlyHint: This panel shows routing inputs only. Live breaker state is available on the Health page.
combos.routingInputsHint: Mode pack and weighting stay here; breaker runtime state stays on the Health page.
combos.emailVisibilityHint: Account emails here follow the global privacy toggle.
combos.emailVisibilityTooltip: Use the eye icon to reveal or hide account emails globally across combos, providers and quota screens.
combos.builderNeedValidName: Define a valid combo name before continuing.
combos.allProvidersHealthy: Providers are reporting healthy routing conditions.
combos.highCircuitBreakerRate: High circuit breaker trip rate detected.
combos.modePackHint: Switch presets to bias the routing engine without rebuilding the combo.
combos.allProvidersEvaluated: No candidate pool configured. All active providers are evaluated at runtime.
combos.excludedProvidersHint: Providers with an OPEN circuit breaker are temporarily excluded from routing.
combos.builderIntelligentDesc: Configure the multi-factor scoring engine for this auto-routing combo.
combos.candidatePoolHint: Select which providers this engine should evaluate. Leave empty to use all active providers.
combos.explorationRateHint: {percent}% of requests can explore non-optimal providers.

priority.title: Fail-safe baseline
priority.description: Use one primary model and keep fallback chain short and reliable.
priority.tip1: Put your most reliable model first.
priority.tip2: Keep 1-2 backup models with similar quality.
priority.tip3: Use safe retries to absorb transient provider failures.

weighted.title: Controlled traffic split
weighted.description: Great for canary rollouts and gradual migration between models.
weighted.tip2: Keep the total at 100% and auto-balance after changes.
weighted.tip3: Monitor success and latency before increasing canary weight.

round-robin.title: Predictable load sharing
round-robin.description: Best when models are equivalent and you need smooth distribution.
round-robin.tip2: Set concurrency limits to avoid burst overload.
round-robin.tip3: Use queue timeout to fail fast under saturation.

random.title: Quick spread with low setup
random.description: Use when you need simple distribution without strict guarantees.
random.tip1: Use models with similar latency profiles.
random.tip2: Keep retries enabled to absorb random misses.
random.tip3: Prefer this for experimentation, not strict SLAs.

least-used.title: Adaptive balancing
least-used.description: Routes to less-used models to reduce hotspots over time.
least-used.tip2: Combine with health checks for safer balancing.
least-used.tip3: Track per-model usage to validate distribution gains.

cost-optimized.title: Budget-first routing
cost-optimized.description: Routes to lower-cost models when pricing metadata is available.
cost-optimized.tip1: Ensure pricing coverage for all selected models.
cost-optimized.tip2: Keep a quality fallback for hard prompts.
cost-optimized.tip3: Use for batch/background jobs where cost is the main KPI.

reset-aware.title: Reset-aware account rotation
reset-aware.description: Balances remaining provider quota against reset timing.
reset-aware.tip1: Use explicit account steps or account-tag routing for providers with quota telemetry.
reset-aware.tip2: Tune session vs weekly weights when short-term exhaustion is more risky.
reset-aware.tip3: Keep the tie band small so equivalent accounts still rotate fairly.

strict-random.title: Shuffle deck distribution
strict-random.description: Each model is used exactly once per cycle before reshuffling.
strict-random.tip1: Use at least 2 models for meaningful distribution.
strict-random.tip3: Ideal for load balancing across multiple API accounts.

fill-first.description: Exhaust provider quotas sequentially before falling back.
fill-first.tip1: Use for quota-based routing with clear fallback chains.
fill-first.tip2: Set quotas accurately to avoid premature fallback.
fill-first.tip3: Works best with providers offering free tiers or credit buckets.
fill-first.title: Quota Exhaustion

auto.description: Route based on real-time scoring for cost, latency, quality, and health.
auto.tip1: Let the engine balance multiple factors automatically.
auto.tip2: Monitor which factors drive routing decisions in logs.
auto.tip3: Use for complex workloads where no single factor dominates.
auto.title: Multi-Factor Optimization

lkgp.description: Route based on historical performance and success patterns.
lkgp.tip1: Requires sufficient request history for accurate predictions.
lkgp.tip2: Good for workloads with consistent model performance patterns.
lkgp.tip3: Monitor prediction accuracy and adjust weights as needed.
lkgp.title: History-Based Routing

context-optimized.description: Optimize routing based on context window usage and token efficiency.
context-optimized.tip1: Route long conversations to models with larger context windows.
context-optimized.tip2: Monitor context utilization to avoid token waste.
context-optimized.tip3: Best for conversational AI requiring extensive context retention.
context-optimized.title: Context Optimization

context-relay.description: Best when account rotation is expected and the next account must inherit a simplified task summary.
context-relay.tip1: Use with providers rotating accounts for the same model family.
context-relay.tip2: Set handoff threshold below hard quota cutoffs for summary generation time.
context-relay.tip3: Only set a dedicated summary model if the primary is too expensive or unstable.
context-relay.title: Session Continuity Priority

p2c.description: Route to the provider with the lowest current load based on real-time metrics.
p2c.tip1: Monitor provider health metrics for accurate load assessment.
p2c.tip2: Works best with homogeneous provider pools.
p2c.tip3: Adjust sensitivity to avoid oscillation during traffic spikes.
p2c.title: Power of Two Choices

combos.templateFreeStackDesc: Round-robin across all free providers: Kiro (Claude), Qoder (5 models), Qwen (4 models), Gemini CLI. Zero cost, never stops coding.
combos.autoDesc: Self-healing smart routing pool with multi-factor scoring
combos.lkgpDesc: Prioritizes the last provider that successfully completed a request

combos.wizardStep2Desc: Select AI models and arrange their fallback priority order

combos.wizardStep4Desc: Review your configuration and activate the combo

intelligent.label: Smart Routing
intelligent.description: Auto-routing candidate pool, presets, and scoring

combos.builderDuplicateExact: This exact provider/model/account step is already in the combo.
combos.builderStagesDescription: Complete each stage in order to define combos, build steps, select routing strategy, and review results.
combos.builderStepsDescription: Build each combo step in order: provider, model, then account. This allows reusing the same provider and model across different accounts.
combos.addStepBeforeContinue: Please add at least one step before proceeding to the next stage.
combos.agentFeaturesDescription: Enable advanced features for agents using this combo
combos.agentFeaturesContextCacheHint: Enable in-context cache for agent tools
combos.agentFeaturesContextCacheProtection: Protect cache from agent tool mutations
combos.agentFeaturesContextLengthHint: Defines the context window for this combo in /v1/models.
combos.agentFeaturesContextLengthErrorInteger: Context length must be a valid integer
combos.agentFeaturesContextLengthErrorRange: Context length must be between 1000 and 2000000

costs.title: Costs
costs.pageDescription: Track spending, analyze trends, and manage your AI budget across all providers
costs.noCostDataDescription: Start making requests to see cost data appear here. Costs are tracked automatically for all providers.
costs.overviewDescription: Real-time spending breakdown across all connected providers and models
costs.costExplorerDescription: Explore spend by provider, model, API key, account, or service tier.
costs.noMatchingCostRowsDescription: Adjust the search text, grouping, or selected time window.

health.title: System Health
health.description: Real-time monitoring of your OmniRoute instance
health.noCBData: No circuit breaker data available. Make some requests first.
health.configuredProvidersHint: Providers with credentials saved in /dashboard/providers, regardless of runtime state.
health.activeProvidersHint: Configured providers currently enabled for routing requests.
health.monitoredProvidersHint: Providers currently tracked by circuit-breaker health monitors.
health.resetConfirm: Reset all circuit breakers to healthy state? This will clear all failure counts and restore all providers to operational status.
health.resetAllTitle: Reset all circuit breakers to healthy state
health.providerHealthMatrixDescription: Provider × account × model states from breakers, cooldowns, lockouts and logs.
health.failedProviderHealthMatrix: Failed to load Provider Health Matrix: {error}
health.noProvidersMatchedFilters: No providers matched the current filters.
health.noSyncedModelsOrTraffic: No synced models or recent traffic yet.

logs.title: Logs
logs.providerWarningDesc: Some providers returned warnings during request processing
logs.runningRequestsDesc: Real-time view of currently running upstream requests
logs.cleanHistoryMessage: This permanently clears all request log rows, legacy detail rows, and local artifact files under DATA_DIR/call_logs. The live page will refresh after cleanup.
logs.cleanHistorySuccess: Cleaned {deleted} log entries, {deletedArtifacts} artifacts, and {deletedDetailedLogs} legacy detail rows.
logs.cleanHistoryEmpty: No request log history was found.

providers.title: Providers
providers.addFirstProviderDesc: Connect an AI provider to start routing requests through OmniRoute. You can use free providers, API keys, or OAuth accounts.
providers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?
providers.testAllModelsConfirm: Test all visible models? Failed models will be hidden if auto-hide is enabled.
providers.noModelsToTest: No models match the current filter
providers.autoHideFailedHint: When enabled, Test all hides non-transient failures from public catalogs such as /v1/models. Single-model tests never auto-hide.
providers.freeAggregated: All providers with free tier (also shown in their native category)

riskNotice.title: Before continuing
riskNotice.tooltip: Provider with usage caveats — click for details
riskNotice.oauth:
This provider uses your official product session/OAuth, which is not authorized for proxy/router use.

We don't recommend intensive autonomous agent usage (OpenCloud-style, long multi-step flows, large batches) — the upstream may react by restricting or banning the account.

Use at your own risk.
riskNotice.webCookie:
This provider authenticates through your web session cookies. The upstream service may invalidate the session at any time, requiring you to log in again. Not recommended for long unattended operations.

Use at your own risk.
riskNotice.deprecated: This provider was discontinued by its upstream and may stop working without notice. Existing connections may continue working until the upstream fully removes access. Consider alternatives.
riskNotice.dontShowAgain: Don't show this again for this provider

providers.openRouterAnyModelHint: OpenRouter supports any model. Add models and create aliases for quick access.

providers.normalizeToolCallIdLabel: Normalize tool call IDs to 9 characters (e.g. Mistral)
providers.preserveDeveloperRoleLabel: Keep OpenAI Responses developer role (do not map to system)
providers.compatProtocolHint: These options apply when OmniRoute detects this request shape (OpenAI Chat, Responses API, or Anthropic Messages).
providers.compatUpstreamHeadersHint: High-privilege setting — same trust level as editing provider API credentials; only trusted admins should use it. Merged after OmniRoute adds auth from the provider API key. If a custom header uses the same name as an existing one (e.g. Authorization), your value fully replaces the auto-generated header (including the Bearer token) — the upstream only sees what you typed, not the key from settings. Misconfiguration can cause 401 or broken upstream auth. One row per header (e.g. extra Authentication for some gateways). Hover or focus the value to preview. Saves on blur, outside click, or closing this panel.
providers.perModelQuotaDescription: When enabled, 429/404 errors only lock the specific model, not the entire connection. Use for providers with per-model rate limits (e.g., ModelScope).
providers.importFreeModelsOnlyHint: When enabled, only this provider's free models are imported. Paid models are skipped.

providers.geminiImportIdentityUnverified: Could not verify identity from id_token. Enable "Replace existing" or provide an email.
providers.geminiImportBulkUploadHint: Drop or pick up to 50 oauth_creds.json files (256KB each, 10MB total).
providers.geminiImportBulkPasteHint: Paste an array of objects: [{ json, name?, email? }, ...]
providers.geminiImportBulkZipHint: ZIP containing oauth_creds.json entries. Max 50 entries, 10MB unpacked.
providers.codexAuthApplyFailed: Failed to apply Codex auth.json locally
providers.codexExternalLinkModalDescription: Share this single-use link with the person who will authenticate the Codex account. They open it in their own browser, complete the OpenAI login, and the connection is registered here. The link expires in 15 minutes.
providers.codexExternalLinkWaiting: Waiting for browser authentication. This window refreshes automatically.
providers.codexExternalLinkConnected: Codex account connected through the external link.
providers.codexImportFileHint: Select the auth.json file exported from Codex or from OmniRoute.
providers.codexImportEmailHint: Auto-detected from the file; edit if needed.
providers.codexImportOverwriteLabel: Replace existing connection if account already exists
providers.codexImportInvalidJson: Could not parse the file as JSON
providers.codexImportInvalidShape: The file is not a valid Codex auth.json
providers.codexImportDuplicate: Account already exists — enable "Replace existing" to overwrite
providers.codexImportBulkUploadHint: Select multiple .json files or drag and drop
providers.codexImportBulkPasteHint: JSON array [ {...}, {...} ] or multiple JSONs separated by --- on its own line
providers.codexImportBulkZipHint: Upload a .zip containing auth.json files (max 50 files, 10 MB)
providers.chatPathHint: Custom chat path for providers with non-standard APIs (e.g. /v4/chat/completions)
providers.addAnotherApiKey: Add another API key or paste multiple keys
providers.azureOpenAiBaseUrlHint: Azure Open Ai Base Url Hint
providers.claudeWebCookieHint: Open claude.ai → DevTools → Application → Cookies → claude.ai, copy the 'sessionKey' value. Also need cf_clearance, __cf_bm, _cfuvid for Cloudflare.
providers.t3ChatWebCookieHint: Open t3.chat → DevTools → Application → Local Storage → https://t3.chat, copy 'convex-session-id'. Then open DevTools → Network, copy the full Cookie header from any chat request. Paste both values in the fields below.
providers.blockClaudeExtraUsageDescription: Hide extra Claude usage rows reported by some providers when they duplicate primary token accounting.
providers.disableCoolingDescription: Skip the transient cooldown so this connection stays eligible even after recoverable errors (terminal states like banned/expired still apply).
providers.bulkPasteAdded: {count, plural, one {1 key added} other {# keys added}}
providers.bulkPasteDuplicatesIgnored: {count, plural, one {1 duplicate skipped} other {# duplicates skipped}}
providers.bulkPasteHint: Paste one API key per line. Empty lines are ignored and duplicate keys are skipped.
providers.ccCompatibleBaseUrlHint: Base URL for a Claude Code-only relay. Do not include /messages.
providers.ccCompatibleChatPathHint: Defaults to Claude Code's strict Messages API path. Change only if your relay documents a different path.
providers.ccCompatibleContext1mDescription: Adds the context-1m beta header when the selected Claude model supports it.
providers.ccCompatibleRedactThinkingDescription: Adds the redact-thinking beta header for CC Compatible upstreams that require hidden Claude thinking streams.
providers.ccCompatibleModelsDescription: CC-compatible relays do not expose model listing. Add the Claude model IDs your relay accepts.
providers.ccCompatibleNameHint: Display name for this Claude Code-only relay.
providers.ccCompatiblePrefixHint: Used in model aliases such as prefix/model-id.
providers.ccCompatibleValidationHint: Use this provider only for relays that serve Claude Code clients exclusively. OmniRoute rewrites any incoming request into the Claude Code-compatible wire format so those relays accept it. If you only want to use Claude Code CLI, or you are not sure what this relay type means, use a regular Anthropic-compatible provider instead.
providers.claudeExtraUsageToggleTitle: Block Claude extra usage accounting for this connection
providers.codex5hToggleTitle: Track Codex 5-hour quota for this connection
providers.codexFastServiceTierDescription: Use the priority service tier for Codex requests when available.
providers.codexWeeklyToggleTitle: Track Codex weekly quota for this connection
providers.defaultThinkingStrengthHint: Used when a request does not specify reasoning effort.
providers.apiKeyInvalidAlert: {count} API key(s) marked as invalid due to authentication failures in connections: {connections}. They will be skipped in rotation. Click to review.
providers.apiKeyWarningAlert: {count} API key(s) in warning state due to elevated failure rate in connections: {connections}. Review to prevent rotation issues.
providers.geminiCliProjectIdHint: Your Google Cloud Project ID. Required for accounts with exceptions. Enter your GCP Project ID to use with Gemini CLI.
providers.antigravityProjectIdHint: Optional override for Antigravity Cloud Code requests. Leave blank to use the project discovered during Google OAuth.
providers.antigravityClientProfileHint: Choose which Antigravity client identity OmniRoute presents to the API.
providers.localProviderApiKeyOptionalHint: Local Provider Api Key Optional Hint
providers.openaiResponsesStoreDescription: Allow compatible Responses API requests to preserve stored response state.
providers.rateLimitOverridesMaxConcurrentHint: Max concurrent requests override for this connection. Overrides the account-level cap.
providers.rateLimitOverridesMinTimeHint: Minimum time (ms) between requests. Overrides the default rate limiter delay.
providers.rateLimitOverridesRpmHint: Max requests per minute for this connection. Overrides the provider default.
providers.rateLimitOverridesTpdHint: Max tokens per day for this connection. Overrides the provider default.
providers.rateLimitOverridesTpmHint: Max tokens per minute for this connection. Overrides the provider default.
providers.totalKeysRotating: {count, plural, one {1 key rotating} other {# keys rotating}}
providers.vertexServiceAccountPlaceholder: Paste your Service Account JSON ({"type":"service_account","project_id":"…","client_email":"…","private_key":"…"}) or an OAuth access_token
providers.freeTierProvidersDesc: Providers with free tiers — some require an API key signup, others need no credentials at all.
providers.ideProvidersDesc: Editors with built-in AI subscription. Use the provider page to import credentials directly from the IDE keychain.
providers.noIdeProviders: No IDE providers match the current filters.
providers.providerDetailFastTierTooltip: Apply Codex Fast tier to all Codex connections by default
providers.webFetchTooltip: Providers that extract content from web URLs (HTML → Markdown, scrape, screenshot)
providers.compatibleProvidersDesc: OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible endpoints you host or configure. Point any OpenAI SDK to your own URL and route requests here.
providers.oauthProvidersDesc: Providers authenticated via OAuth — sign in once and OmniRoute handles token rotation automatically.
providers.webCookieProvidersDesc: These providers use browser web sessions, cookies, or web tokens instead of API keys. Open a provider to add the required session credential.
providers.apiKeyProvidersDesc: Standard API key providers. Add your key once and OmniRoute routes, retries, and rate-limits on your behalf.
providers.noAuthProvidersDesc: Open endpoints that require no credentials — ready to use immediately without any sign-up.
providers.upstreamProxyProvidersDesc: Route outbound traffic through an upstream proxy before it reaches the provider. Useful for corporate networks or traffic inspection.
providers.webFetchProvidersDesc: Providers that fetch and extract content from web URLs. Use them to ground prompts with live web data.
providers.aggregatorsGatewaysDesc: Multi-provider aggregators and AI gateways that expose a single unified API across dozens of underlying models.
providers.enterpriseCloudDesc: Enterprise-tier and cloud-hosted models with enhanced SLAs, compliance certifications, and dedicated capacity.
providers.cloudAgentProvidersDesc: Autonomous cloud agents that execute long-running tasks with plan approval and live status tracking.
providers.localProvidersDesc: Self-hosted models running on your own hardware. No data leaves your infrastructure.
providers.onboardingWizardDescription: Connect API-key, custom compatible, and OAuth providers with validation, persistence, and an immediate connection test.
providers.onboardingTypeApiKeyText: Use built-in providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, Azure, and more.
providers.onboardingTypeCustomText: Create an OpenAI-, Anthropic-, or Claude Code-compatible endpoint and add its key.
providers.onboardingTypeOAuthText: Reuse the existing OAuth, device-code, or local import flows for coding providers.
providers.onboardingChooseProviderDescription: Select a provider, then the wizard will guide you through credentials and testing.
providers.onboardingOAuthNoConnectionFound: OAuth finished, but no provider connection was found.
providers.onboardingCreateCustomCompatibleDescript: The wizard creates a provider node first, then stores and tests its API-key connection.
providers.onboardingProviderPrefixHint: Used to generate the managed provider id.
providers.onboardingOAuthFlowDescription: OmniRoute will open the existing OAuth flow for this provider. After login, the wizard reloads the saved connection and runs the same connection test as the provider page.
providers.passthroughModelsDescription: {provider} accepts provider-native model IDs. Import from /models or add custom IDs for routing.
providers.bedrockModelsDescription: Amazon Bedrock models are scoped by AWS region. Import from /models or add Bedrock model IDs enabled in the selected region.
providers.webCookieCredentialHint: Required cookie: {credential}. Paste the Cookie header value from your own signed-in {provider} web session. Do not include the Cookie: prefix.
providers.webTokenCredentialHint: Credential: {credential}. Paste the token value from your own signed-in {provider} web session, or a DevTools HAR export if the provider supports it.
providers.webCookieEditHint: Leave blank to keep the current session cookie. Required cookie: {credential}.
providers.webTokenEditHint: Leave blank to keep the current web session token. Credential: {credential}.
providers.webSessionGuideTitle: How to get the session credential
providers.webSessionGuideIntro: {provider} uses a browser web session instead of an API key.
providers.webSessionGuideStep1: Sign in to {provider} in your browser.
providers.webSessionGuideStep2: Open the browser developer tools and inspect a request made by the web app.
providers.webSessionGuideStep3: Copy the required credential from the provider's own domain. For cookies, copy only the Cookie header value and omit Cookie:.
providers.webSessionGuideStep4: Paste it here and check the connection. If it stops working, sign in again and replace it with a fresh value.
providers.webSessionSecurityHint: Treat this like a password: it may access your signed-in web account until it expires or is revoked.
providers.webNoAuthGuideBody: {provider} does not need an API key or cookie. Save the connection to use its free web endpoint.
providers.webSessionCredentialValidationFailed: Session credential validation failed. Sign in again, copy a fresh credential, and try again.
providers.kimiWebDesc: Chinese market AI chat via kimi.moonshot.cn
providers.claudeApplyOAuthHint: Existing Claude OAuth state will be preserved.

settings.settingsSidebarDesc: Control which items appear in the sidebar and their order
settings.sidebarPresetsDesc: The minimal source profile is the only preset in this branch.
settings.presetConfirmWarning: Applying this preset will replace your current visibility and order settings.
settings.sidebarOrderDesc: Toggle items on/off and drag to reorder sections and their entries.
settings.sidebarVisibilityHint: A sidebar section hides automatically when all of its entries are hidden

auth.unifiedAiApiProxyDesc: Route requests to multiple AI providers through a single endpoint. Load balancing, failover, and usage tracking built in.
auth.errorOccurredRetry: An error occurred. Please try again.
auth.configureInstance: Let's get your OmniRoute instance configured
auth.runOnboardingWizard: Run the onboarding wizard to set up your password and connect your first AI provider.
auth.setPasswordDescription: Set a password to protect your dashboard and secure your API endpoints from unauthorized access.
auth.closeTabNow: You can close this tab now.
auth.copyUrlManual: Please copy the URL from the address bar and paste it in the application.
auth.accessDeniedDescription: You don't have permission to access this resource. Check your API key or contact the administrator.
auth.resetDescription: Choose a method to recover access to your dashboard
auth.pleaseWait: Please wait while we complete the authorization.
auth.methodCliDescription: Run the following command on the server where OmniRoute is running:
auth.methodCliHint: This will prompt you to set a new password. The server must be stopped first.
auth.methodManualDescription: Delete the password from the database and set a new one on startup:
auth.setPasswordInYour: Set a new password in your
auth.restartServerWithNewPassword: Restart the server - it will use the new password
auth.defaultPasswordHint: Default password: CHANGEME (unless INITIAL_PASSWORD was set)
auth.nodeIncompatibleDesc: You are running Node.js {version}, which is outside OmniRoute's supported secure runtime policy. Use a patched Node.js 20.x or 22.x LTS release.
auth.nodeIncompatibleFixLabel: Fix: install a patched Node.js 22 LTS release
auth.nodeIncompatibleHint: OmniRoute requires Node.js 20.20.2+ (20.x LTS), 22.22.2+ (22.x LTS) or 24.0.0+ (24.x LTS). Node 24 LTS is recommended.

proxyConfigModal.errorSelectSavedProxy: Please select a saved proxy first.
proxyConfigModal.errorSocks5Hidden: SOCKS5 is configured but hidden because NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_SOCKS5_PROXY=false.

oauthModal.title: Connect {providerName}
oauthModal.popupClosedHint: If the popup closes or cannot relay the callback, this dialog will auto-switch to manual URL entry mode.
oauthModal.deviceCodeVisitUrl: Visit the URL below and enter code:
oauthModal.googleOAuthWarning: Remote access + Google OAuth: bundled credentials only accept loopback redirects like 127.0.0.1. The browser that approves Google must be able to reach OmniRoute on that local port, usually by opening OmniRoute locally or using an SSH/local-forward tunnel. For fully remote use without this local callback, configure your own OAuth credentials.
oauthModal.remoteAccessInfo: Remote access: Since you're accessing OmniRoute remotely, after authorization you'll see an error page (localhost not found). This is normal — just copy the full URL from your browser address bar and paste it below.
oauthModal.step1OpenUrl: Step 1: Open this URL in your browser
oauthModal.step2PasteCallback: Step 2: Paste callback URL or authorization code here
oauthModal.step2Hint: After authorization, paste the full callback URL. For Claude Code and Cline, you can also paste the authentication code directly, e.g. code#state.

cursorAuthModal.title: Connect Cursor IDE
cursorAuthModal.readingFromCursor: Reading from Cursor IDE or cursor-agent
cursorAuthModal.tokensAutoDetected: Tokens successfully auto-detected from Cursor IDE!
cursorAuthModal.cursorNotDetected: Cursor IDE not detected. Please manually paste your token.

requestLogger.capturePipeline: Capture pipeline payloads for new requests
requestLogger.searchPlaceholder: Search models, providers, accounts, API keys, combos...
requestLogger.noLogs: No logs yet. Make some API calls to see them here.
requestLogger.callLogsInfo: Call logs are also saved as JSON files to {dataDir} and rotated based on {retentionDays} and {maxEntries}.
